summary.description: Review verdict and rationale. Do not restate the PR diff, maintainer next action, risk list, or best-solution text.

changeSummary.description: Neutral one-sentence summary of the requested change or PR diff. For pull requests, describe what the branch changes, not the merge verdict, maintainer next action, risk, or whether the PR is redundant.

evidence.description: Evidence used for the decision. For implemented_on_main and mostly_implemented_on_main, include source evidence with file+sha, git-history provenance from git blame/log/show, and release or current-main-only provenance.

command.description: Exact read-only command used, when applicable. Implementation close decisions need at least one git blame/log/show command and one release or current-main-only check.

sha.description: Commit SHA supporting this evidence. Source and provenance evidence for implementation close decisions must include a SHA.

likelyOwners.description: People most likely connected to the relevant code or behavior, based on git blame/log/PR history. Include multiple candidates when responsibility is shared or ambiguous.

person.description: GitHub login, maintainer handle, or commit author display name. Do not include email addresses.

role.description: Neutral role in the history, such as introduced behavior, recent maintainer, reviewer, merger, adjacent owner, or likely follow-up owner.

reason.description: Brief evidence-backed reason this person is relevant.

bestSolution.description: The best possible product/code/docs direction for this item, whether the item should close or stay open. Do not repeat the change summary, workReason, or risk list.

triagePriority.description: ClawSweeper-maintained triage priority for maintainers to sort issues and pull requests. P0: Emergency: data loss, security bypass, crash loop, or unusable core runtime. P1: Urgent regression or broken agent/channel workflow affecting real users now. P2: Normal priority bug or improvement with limited blast radius. P3: Low-risk cleanup, docs, polish, ergonomics, or speculative feature. Use none only when no priority label should be applied. Do not raise priority solely because CI or status checks are failing, pending, missing, flaky, or require routine maintainer follow-up; use check state as priority evidence only for user-facing automation failures or diff-caused urgent regressions.

impactLabels.description: ClawSweeper-owned GitHub impact labels for maintainers to find the affected problem class on issues. Use [] for pull requests and when no meaningful owned impact signal applies. These describe what the issue is about, not PR merge risk. Use at most 3; impact:other counts toward this max and means meaningful maintainer-visible impact exists but no specific owned impact label fits. Prefer a specific impact label over impact:other. impact:data-loss: This issue is about lost, corrupted, or silently dropped user/session/config data. impact:security: This issue is about security boundaries, credentials, authz, sandboxing, or sensitive data. impact:crash-loop: This issue is about crashes, hangs, restart loops, or process-level availability. impact:message-loss: This issue is about lost, duplicated, misrouted, or suppressed channel messages. impact:session-state: This issue is about session, memory, transcript, context, or agent state drift. impact:auth-provider: This issue is about auth, provider routing, model choice, or SecretRef resolution. impact:other: This issue has meaningful maintainer-visible impact outside the owned taxonomy.

mergeRiskLabels.description: PR-only ClawSweeper-owned GitHub labels for merge risks that green CI does not settle. These describe what could go wrong specifically because this PR is merged, not the affected or solved problem class. Use at most 3 for pull requests and [] for issues and when no meaningful owned merge-risk signal applies. Do not use merge-risk: 🚨 automation only because CI is red, pending, flaky, or absent; use it only when the PR diff changes automation behavior or plausibly causes CI, automerge, proof capture, label sync, or related automation to fail after merge. merge-risk: 🚨 other counts toward this max and means meaningful merge risk exists but no specific owned merge-risk label fits. Prefer a specific merge-risk label over merge-risk: 🚨 other. merge-risk: 🚨 compatibility: 🚨 Merging this PR could break existing users, config, migrations, defaults, or upgrades. merge-risk: 🚨 message-delivery: 🚨 Merging this PR could drop, duplicate, misroute, suppress, or wrongly target messages. merge-risk: 🚨 session-state: 🚨 Merging this PR could lose, corrupt, stale, or mis-associate session or agent state. merge-risk: 🚨 auth-provider: 🚨 Merging this PR could break OAuth, tokens, provider routing, model choice, or credentials. merge-risk: 🚨 security-boundary: 🚨 Merging this PR could weaken sandboxing, authorization, credentials, or sensitive data. merge-risk: 🚨 availability: 🚨 Merging this PR could cause crashes, hangs, restart loops, stalls, or process outages. merge-risk: 🚨 automation: 🚨 Merging this PR could break CI, automerge, proof capture, label sync, or automation. merge-risk: 🚨 other: 🚨 Merging this PR has meaningful risk outside the owned taxonomy.

body.description: One concrete sentence explaining this maintainer option.

recommended.description: True for at most one option, only when the evidence supports a clear best path.

automergeInstruction.description: Only for a recommended fix_before_merge option suitable for automation; otherwise empty. This must be only the special-instructions payload, without @clawsweeper automerge, @clawsweeper autofix, or this PR command text.

mergeRiskOptions.description: Risk-specific maintainer options for the Risk before merge section. Use [] when mergeRiskLabels is []. When mergeRiskLabels is non-empty, provide 1-3 prose-first options. Do not use a fixed menu: tailor titles and bodies to this PR. Mark at most one option recommended, and only when evidence supports a clear best path. Include pause_or_close when the PR may not be worth the risk. Put only the special-instructions payload in automergeInstruction for a recommended fix_before_merge option suitable for automation; do not include @clawsweeper automerge, @clawsweeper autofix, or this PR command text.

value.description: Concrete count or measured change summary, such as 2 added, 1 changed, 0 removed; 1 workflow changed; or 3 files affected.

reason.description: One concise sentence explaining why this measured fact matters to maintainers before merge.

reviewMetrics.description: Concise quantified PR review facts for the top-level Review metrics digest. Emit [] unless a metric is useful, concrete, maintainer-relevant, and grounded in the diff, current behavior, repository policy, or discussion. Do not use vague metric labels or values.

reason.description: One concise maintainer-facing sentence explaining why this exact selected label applies.

labelJustifications.description: One justification for every selected ClawSweeper-managed triage, impact, and merge-risk label. Include the selected triagePriority unless it is none, every selected impactLabels entry, and every selected mergeRiskLabels entry. Do not include labels that were not selected.

itemCategory.description: Primary item category. Use bug only for broken existing behavior with an already-defined expected behavior; use skill for optional skill bundles or skill-only pull requests; feature/config/product changes are not bugs.

reproductionStatus.description: Structured reproduction state. Use reproduced only when there is a high-confidence current-main reproduction path for a bug.

requiresNewFeature.description: True when solving the item would add a new user-facing capability or mode instead of fixing broken existing behavior.

requiresNewConfigOption.description: True when solving the item would require adding a config option, CLI flag, setting, environment variable, or policy knob.

requiresProductDecision.description: True when solving the item requires maintainer product/UX/policy judgment before implementation.

reproductionAssessment.description: Answer this exact question for public review details: Do we have a high-confidence way to reproduce the issue? Include yes/no/unclear/not applicable and the evidence-backed reason.

solutionAssessment.description: Answer this exact question for public review details: Is this the best way to solve the issue? Include yes/no/unclear/not applicable and the evidence-backed rationale or safer alternative.

visionFit.description: For issues, compare the requested work against the target repository VISION.md when present. Use aligned only when the issue fits current priorities or explicit future priorities and does not conflict with roadmap guardrails; rejected when VISION.md says the work should live elsewhere or not merge for now; unclear when evidence is mixed or VISION.md is unavailable; not_applicable for pull requests and non-product cleanup.

visionFitReason.description: One concise evidence-backed reason for the visionFit value. Mention VISION.md when it was inspected.

visionFitEvidence.description: Short VISION.md or repository-policy evidence strings supporting the vision-fit decision. Use [] when not applicable.

implementationComplexity.description: Estimated implementation size for an autonomous issue-to-PR attempt. Use small only for one focused PR with clear files and validation; medium when several areas are touched but still bounded; large for broad architecture, multi-package, migration, or cross-product work; unclear when the shape is not known; not_applicable for PRs and close decisions.

autoImplementationCandidate.description: Whether this issue should be eligible for automatic PR intake after review. Use strict_bug only for the existing reproduced-bug lane. Use vision_fit only for high-confidence, VISION.md-aligned, small, queue_fix_pr issue work with clear validation and no security/product-decision blocker. Otherwise use none.

rootCauseCluster.description: Read-only root-cause relationship assessment. This does not dispatch repair, suppress jobs, mutate sibling items, close, or merge anything. Use fixed_by_candidate only when one side of that relationship is a pull request: an issue may be fixed by a canonical PR, or a PR may be the candidate fix for a canonical issue.

agentsPolicyStatus.description: Structured status for the target repository AGENTS.md review policy check. Report whether AGENTS.md was found, read fully, and applied to this review without duplicating policy text.

found.description: True when a target repository AGENTS.md was found.

readFully.description: True only when the full target repository AGENTS.md was read, not a snippet or truncated copy.

applied.description: True when relevant AGENTS.md guidance affected the review.

summary.description: One concise public-safe sentence summarizing the AGENTS.md policy status.

reviewFindings.description: Codex /review-style PR findings introduced by the proposed patch. Use an empty array for issues, close decisions, or PRs where there is no discrete actionable bug.

title.description: Short imperative title, at most 80 characters.

body.description: Brief Markdown explanation of the concrete bug and why the author should fix it.

file.description: Repository-relative file path for the changed line.

securityReview.description: Dedicated security and supply-chain review for the item. For PRs, summarize whether the diff introduces security-sensitive concerns. For issues, use not_applicable unless the issue itself is security-sensitive.

summary.description: One concise sentence explaining the security review result.

concerns.description: Concrete security or supply-chain concerns found during the dedicated pass. Empty when cleared or not applicable.

title.description: Short concern title, at most 80 characters.

body.description: Brief explanation of the observable security risk and why it matters.

file.description: Repository-relative file path when tied to a file; null for issue-wide or discussion-only concerns.

line.description: Relevant line when tied to a specific file; null when not applicable.

realBehaviorProof.description: Dedicated PR proof assessment. For external PRs, after-fix evidence from a real setup is required unless the PR only changes files under docs/; tests, mocks, snapshots, lint, typechecks, and CI are supplemental only.

summary.description: One concise sentence explaining whether the PR body contains after-fix real behavior proof. Do not call screenshot-only proof sufficient for browser runtime, CSP, CORS, network, or security changes when it only claims no console error or violation is visible.

evidenceKind.description: Use screenshot only for directly visible UI or flow proof. For browser runtime, CSP, CORS, network, or security proof, prefer terminal, logs, live_output, or linked_artifact unless the media visibly includes the diagnostic output.

needsContributorAction.description: True when the contributor must add or improve real behavior proof before merge. This must be false for sufficient, override, and not_applicable statuses.

prRating.description: Calibrated PR quality rating. Use boring internal tiers S/A/B/C/D/F/NA; public rendering maps them to funny crustacean labels. Rate the PR evidence and patch quality, not the contributor or workflow state. S is rare and exceptional, A is clearly above average, B is the normal good/mergeable quality rating, C is useful but uncertain, D is weak/thin signal, F is not quality-ready, and NA is only for non-PR or not-applicable items. Real screenshots, recordings, or linked media that directly show the changed behavior are strong boosters, but screenshots alone are not enough for browser runtime, network, CSP, or security claims unless diagnostics are visible. Do not lower any tier solely because the PR is draft, has protected labels, is not automerge-eligible, or is waiting on a maintainer decision.

proofTier.description: Rating for real behavior proof quality only.

patchTier.description: Rating for implementation quality, review findings, security concerns, scope, and validation.

overallTier.description: Overall PR quality tier after combining proof and patch quality. Missing, mock-only, or insufficient proof must cap or lower the overall rating because real behavior proof remains a merge gate. Draft, protected-label, automerge eligibility, and maintainer-waiting workflow states must not lower this tier by themselves.

summary.description: One concise sentence explaining the calibrated overall rating without insulting the contributor.

nextSteps.description: Zero to three concrete, merge-relevant rank-up moves. Use an empty array for S/A/NA and for B when there is no material risk reducer. Do not invent optional polish work.

telegramVisibleProof.description: For Telegram PRs, classify whether the change is user-visible and easy to demonstrate with the telegram-crabbox-e2e-proof skill.

summary.description: One concise sentence explaining why the PR should or should not receive the mantis: telegram-visible-proof label.

mantisRecommendation.description: For PRs, recommend a copy-paste maintainer comment for OpenClaw Mantis only when live transport or visual proof would materially help review. Do not claim Mantis has run and do not dispatch any workflow.

reason.description: One concise sentence explaining why this Mantis lane would or would not add useful maintainer proof.

maintainerComment.description: When recommended, a single-line PR comment starting with @openclaw-mantis that a maintainer can paste. ClawSweeper validates and renders this mention inside a fenced text block so the public recommendation is directly copyable without starting Mantis workflows. Do not use shorter or ambiguous Mantis account mentions. For not_recommended, use an empty string.

featureShowcase.description: Positive-only maintainer spotlight for unusually compelling feature ideas in PRs. This is not a merge-readiness score, not a contributor ranking, and not a merge gate. Use showcase only when the PR is a feature or adds a new user-facing capability and the idea is exceptional enough that maintainers should notice it even if ordinary review work remains. Use none for normal useful features.

reason.description: One concise maintainer-facing sentence explaining the idea-level reason for the feature spotlight, or why no spotlight applies. Do not mention contributor worth.

overallCorrectness.description: Codex /review-style overall patch verdict. Use not a patch for issues and non-PR items.

fixedRelease.description: Release tag/version where the issue was fixed, if known. Use null when not applicable or not known.

fixedSha.description: Specific commit SHA that fixed or proves the issue is implemented. Required for implemented_on_main and mostly_implemented_on_main; keep open if this cannot be identified.

fixedAt.description: ISO-8601 commit or merge timestamp for the fix/proof SHA when known. Required for implemented_on_main and mostly_implemented_on_main when fixedRelease is null so maintainers know when it became fixed on main.

workCandidate.description: Whether this item should become a manually promoted ClawSweeper repair job. Use queue_fix_pr only for concrete actionable repairs; use manual_review or none for maintainer/product/security review or normal PR validation.

workReason.description: Short reason this item is or is not a safe fix-PR candidate. For pull requests that need human handling, make this the maintainer next action and do not restate bestSolution.

workPrompt.description: Maintainer prompt to hand to the ClawSweeper repair lane if the item is promoted.
